pdf_extraction_process: Extract antibacterial peptide MIC records from open-access PDFs via pdfplumber text/table parsing driven by scripts/extract_pdf.py.
extraction_note: Place PDF files in data/raw/pdf/ exactly as pdf_path names in input_sources below. Implemented parsers register in TEXT_PARSERS inside scripts/extract_pdf.py. Every exported row must have a non-empty measurement_value (MIC text from the paper, lightly normalized) and peptide_sequence when the peptide sequence is identifiable from the parsed tables (C14R: sequence taken from Mildenberger et al. 2024, Pharmaceuticals 17:83). Final `data/processed/dataset.csv` stores float-parseable MIC scalars after scripts/clean_dataset.py.

input_sources.selection_reason: Open-access CC BY 4.0. Table 1: peptide sequences; Table 2: 11x6 MIC matrix in ug/mL against ESKAPE + C. acnes.
input_sources.expected_content: Table 1 (p.4): peptide names and sequences. Table 2 (p.5): MIC in ug/mL for E. coli ATCC 25922, P. aeruginosa ATCC 27853, K. pneumoniae ATCC 700603, E. faecium ATCC 19434, S. aureus ATCC 25923, C. acnes ATCC 6919.

input_sources.selection_reason: Open-access Microbiology Spectrum. TABLE 1 (p.4): W1-W5 sequences + measured MW. TABLE 2 (p.5): MIC in µM vs MRSA 544 and MRSA 103.
input_sources.expected_content: Peptide table with lines like 'W1 WRGSGRW-NH2'; MIC table labeled TABLE 2 with MRSA 544/MRSA 103 rows.

input_sources.selection_reason: Open-access CC BY 4.0. T. ni and H. cecropia cecropin A MIC vs standard and colistin-resistant strains.
input_sources.expected_content: Table 1 (p.5-6): MIC in uM for cecropin peptides vs 8 bacterial strains. Exclude antibiotic control rows.

input_sources.selection_reason: Open-access MDPI Processes (2026). Table 3 + Table 4 MIC/MBC grids (µg/mL) for melittin, TT-1, FKW, WKW; sequences in Table 2.
input_sources.expected_content: Canonical download name often processes-<vol>-01630.pdf. Parser reads Table 3 (S. aureus ATCC 25923 MIC) and Table 4 MIC columns.

assay_conditions.medium_composition: cation-adjusted Mueller-Hinton broth; 1% DMSO in assay plates

input_sources.selection_reason: Open-access Nature Comm 2024. Table 1 (p.6): 29 peptides (T1-1..T1-18, T2-1..T2-10, Penetratin) MIC in µg/mL vs E.coli, S.aureus, K.pneumoniae, P.aeruginosa, MRSA.
input_sources.expected_content: Table 1 with columns: Peptide Sequence E.coli S.aureus K.pneumoniae P.aeruginosa MRSA (each with ±SEM); ~145 records.

input_sources.selection_reason: Open-access Curr Microbiol 2025. Table 2 (p.6): 12 AI-designed peptides MIC in µM vs E.coli ATCC25922, K.quasipneumoniae ATCC700603, S.aureus ATCC25923, P.aeruginosa ATCC27853.
input_sources.expected_content: Table 2 with columns C.a E.c K.q S.a P.a (skip C.a Candida); ~48 records bacterial.

input_sources.selection_reason: Open-access MDPI Life 2025. Table 1 (p.4): 17 TN/D-TN peptides MIC in µg/mL vs S.aureus ATCC29213, E.coli ATCC25922, P.aeruginosa ATCC27853 (skip C.albicans).
input_sources.expected_content: Table 1 with peptide sequences and MIC values for bacterial strains; ~40 records.

input_sources.selection_reason: Open-access Springer 2025. Table 4 (p.8): 6 SK peptides MIC in µg/mL vs E.coli ATCC8739, S.aureus ATCC6538, K.pneumoniae ATCC700603, P.aeruginosa ATCC9027.
input_sources.expected_content: Table 1 (sequences) on p.3, Table 4 (MIC values) on p.8; ~24 records.

input_sources.selection_reason: Open-access Antibiotics 2025. Table 2 (p.3): B7-005/B7-006/B7-007 MIC in µM vs E.coli ATCC25922 and 2 P.aeruginosa strains.
input_sources.expected_content: Table 1 (sequences) and Table 2 (MIC in µM) for B7-005, B7-006, B7-007 vs 3 reference strains; ~9 records.

input_sources.selection_reason: Open-access Antibiotics 2026. Table 1 (p.4): C14R MIC in µg/mL vs 6 ESKAPE pathogens (MDR strains).
input_sources.expected_content: Table 1: E.faecium VRE, S.aureus MRSA, K.quasipneumoniae ESBL, A.baumannii, P.aeruginosa, E.coli ESBL; ~6 records.
